name: Marshall, Wilson Amis
motto: "Teach me my days to number, and apply My trembling heart to wisdom."
clubs: Phi Sigma Representative in A. & M. Debate
description: A very phenomenal at leading the batting percentage up where it counts—on the Chancellor's record. For instance he made ninety-nine plus in Sophomore Math. Reserved and quiet he goes about minding his own business. Already he has started the study of his chosen profession, Law, and as soon as men find him out they will make a beaten path to his door.
text:
"Teach me my days to number, and apply My trembling heart to wisdom."
A very phenomenal at leading the batting percentage up where it counts—on the Chancellor's record. For instance he made ninety-nine plus in Sophomore Math. Reserved and quiet he goes about minding his own business. Already he has started the study of his chosen profession, Law, and as soon as men find him out they will make a beaten path to his door.

name: Lester, Dent Baggett
motto: "A man in whom there dwells all that is honest and honorable."
description: A man who has labored with great success to make his life what it should be. He has a wonderful "think tank," energy to keep it going, and self-confidence to direct it. Has mastered the "Ancients" and exposed himself to much practical work. His chance will be excellent for success in any of the professions for which these characteristics fit him.
text:
"A man in whom there dwells all that is honest and honorable."
A man who has labored with great success to make his life what it should be. He has a wonderful "think tank," energy to keep it going, and self-confidence to direct it. Has mastered the "Ancients" and exposed himself to much practical work. His chance will be excellent for success in any of the professions for which these characteristics fit him.

name: Kathleen, Baldwin
motto: "Like the river, swift and clear Flows her song through many a heart."
description: The poet of our class. The readers of the Mississippian have been delighted with her writings both in prose and in verse ever since she came from Blue Mountain. Modest and reserved, yet pleasant and courteous, she pursues the even tenor of her way, thinking on deep subjects,—History and German.
text:
"Like the river, swift and clear Flows her song through many a heart."
The poet of our class. The readers of the Mississippian have been delighted with her writings both in prose and in verse ever since she came from Blue Mountain. Modest and reserved, yet pleasant and courteous, she pursues the even tenor of her way, thinking on deep subjects,—History and German.

name: Gladys, Barry
motto: "He saw her charming but he saw not half The charms her down-cast modesty concealed."
clubs: Chairman, Executive Committee of Greek Club
description: Never satisfied unless she ranks number one in every course she is taking, and never does she seem to be troubled whether it be Bondy's Latin, Senior German, or Dr. Tanner's Chemistry. During our whole stay here, we have never found her superior in thorough scholarship. If you don't know, go to her. She does.
text:
"He saw her charming but he saw not half The charms her down-cast modesty concealed."
Never satisfied unless she ranks number one in every course she is taking, and never does she seem to be troubled whether it be Bondy's Latin, Senior German, or Dr. Tanner's Chemistry. During our whole stay here, we have never found her superior in thorough scholarship. If you don't know, go to her. She does.

name: Bean, George
motto: "I woke one morning and found myself famous."
description: Here he is—that famous athlete. For four seasons he has been at the receiving end of his class team's battery. Not afraid of hard work, for he spent three long years under Dr. Bondurant. In his Senior year deserted Lit for Law, and is now telling the Lawyers about contracts and torts "Lem me tell you." And versatile—yes, he is, for he is some chemist: "Shuh, doctor, that ain't nothing but high life." "Buy me something."
text:
"I woke one morning and found myself famous."
Here he is—that famous athlete. For four seasons he has been at the receiving end of his class team's battery. Not afraid of hard work, for he spent three long years under Dr. Bondurant. In his Senior year deserted Lit for Law, and is now telling the Lawyers about contracts and torts "Lem me tell you." And versatile—yes, he is, for he is some chemist: "Shuh, doctor, that ain't nothing but high life." "Buy me something."

name: Brown, Annie Lou
motto: "Thy Modesty's a candle to thy merit."
description: Her one ambition throughout her college career has been to get better grades than Gladys. Whether she has succeeded in this or not it would be hard to say. It is sufficient to say that she has been next to her rival—the question is, on which side.
text:
"Thy Modesty's a candle to thy merit."
Her one ambition throughout her college career has been to get better grades than Gladys. Whether she has succeeded in this or not it would be hard to say. It is sufficient to say that she has been next to her rival—the question is, on which side.

name: Cassibry, Napoleon Lep
motto: "We can say nothing but what hath been said—He that comes last is commonly best."
description: "Nap" came to us this year from Millsaps and has taken a front rank place among us. He is a man easy to get acquainted with, easier to like, and easiest to get along with. He will return next year to take our law course, and from his painstaking work in the classroom we feel safe in predicting that he will be one of the foremost members of the Mississippi bar to go out from the University next year.
text:
"We can say nothing but what hath been said—He that comes last is commonly best."
"Nap" came to us this year from Millsaps and has taken a front rank place among us. He is a man easy to get acquainted with, easier to like, and easiest to get along with. He will return next year to take our law course, and from his painstaking work in the classroom we feel safe in predicting that he will be one of the foremost members of the Mississippi bar to go out from the University next year.

name: Osmond, L. Clark
motto: "Every person is responsible for all the good within the scope of his abilities, and for no more, and none can tell whose sphere is the largest."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Delegate to State Conference 1914
description: His sphere of usefulness extends all over the school, and he has as much genuine college spirit as any man on the campus. He is as steady as a clock, and as true as truth. He believes in meeting his fellowman on the square and leaving him on the level. Osmond is always ready for any duty, and, when he finishes, it has been successfully done.
text:
"Every person is responsible for all the good within the scope of his abilities, and for no more, and none can tell whose sphere is the largest."
His sphere of usefulness extends all over the school, and he has as much genuine college spirit as any man on the campus. He is as steady as a clock, and as true as truth. He believes in meeting his fellowman on the square and leaving him on the level. Osmond is always ready for any duty, and, when he finishes, it has been successfully done.

name: George, Roudebush Culley
motto: "Linked sweetness long drawn out."
description: If "Sawney" can "put them by" out in the world as he has done to many batters on opposing teams, we shall see him "cutting the corners" to success and gathering in his chances with a 1,000 per cent. He has carried all the available Latin and Greek away with him.
text:
"Linked sweetness long drawn out."
If "Sawney" can "put them by" out in the world as he has done to many batters on opposing teams, we shall see him "cutting the corners" to success and gathering in his chances with a 1,000 per cent. He has carried all the available Latin and Greek away with him.

name: Simon, R. Dean
motto: "Take him for all in all, you will not find his like again."
clubs: Class Poet of Junior Medical Class 1913-14
description: The mourning, wailing, suffering, sick of this generation will find in this man a sympathetic monetary extractor of the latest double-compound reflex-action type. It is certain that he will set up an undertaking establishment in connection with his medical practice.
text:
"Take him for all in all, you will not find his like again."
The mourning, wailing, suffering, sick of this generation will find in this man a sympathetic monetary extractor of the latest double-compound reflex-action type. It is certain that he will set up an undertaking establishment in connection with his medical practice.

name: Morgan, Shell Evans
motto: "He stands for the best and his stay with us has been an inspiration."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Jr. Medical Class
Secretary and Treasurer Junior Medical Class
description: Four years have brought about a marked change in Morgan Shell. He has risen from an ignorant Freshman to an Assistant in Anatomy. This is a sufficient explanation to show what his University career has meant to him—though what he has meant to us will not be told for it is in deeds that will live as long as memory.
text:
"He stands for the best and his stay with us has been an inspiration."
Four years have brought about a marked change in Morgan Shell. He has risen from an ignorant Freshman to an Assistant in Anatomy. This is a sufficient explanation to show what his University career has meant to him—though what he has meant to us will not be told for it is in deeds that will live as long as memory.

name: Farley, Nannie
motto: "A fairer face I ne'er looked upon."
description: For two years in succession she has been voted the prettiest co-ed by the student body, and her attractiveness is not confined to her beauty, for she is graceful and pleasant, though quiet and modest. She is studious and thoughtful, and such as she are the best justification of co-education. She is but a fair representative of that noble Farley family from whom the University has been favored with several most brilliant students.
text:
"A fairer face I ne'er looked upon."
For two years in succession she has been voted the prettiest co-ed by the student body, and her attractiveness is not confined to her beauty, for she is graceful and pleasant, though quiet and modest. She is studious and thoughtful, and such as she are the best justification of co-education. She is but a fair representative of that noble Farley family from whom the University has been favored with several most brilliant students.

name: Forbus, Lady Willie
motto: "Work, courage, honor, these indeed your sustenance and birthright are."
description: Lady Willie came among us and made friends with boys and girls alike, by her honest, open-hearted manner. She graduates after three years of work, and we predict for her a brilliant future in whatever sphere of life she chooses to dwell.
text:
"Work, courage, honor, these indeed your sustenance and birthright are."
Lady Willie came among us and made friends with boys and girls alike, by her honest, open-hearted manner. She graduates after three years of work, and we predict for her a brilliant future in whatever sphere of life she chooses to dwell.

name: Gathings, J. R.
motto: "One who never turns his back, but marches breast forward."
description: We gained our greatest victory over Millsaps when we enticed this man from there. He has been at "Ole Miss" only one year but is one of her most popular men.
text:
"One who never turns his back, but marches breast forward."
We gained our greatest victory over Millsaps when we enticed this man from there. He has been at "Ole Miss" only one year but is one of her most popular men.

name: George, James A.
motto: "The only way to have a friend is to be one."
description: We cannot boast of a more earnest and painstaking student. This year he has gone over to read Blackstone with Dr. Farley, and we hear that the habits formed in the academic department are staying with him. A loyal and devoted disciple of Dr. Riley's. "Well, you see it is this way, Dr. Riley." Believes that Tennyson should have said, not "In the spring, a young man's fancy, etc.," but rather "In the summer," when there's nothing else to do. Harry Bryan: "Going to town?"
text:
"The only way to have a friend is to be one."
We cannot boast of a more earnest and painstaking student. This year he has gone over to read Blackstone with Dr. Farley, and we hear that the habits formed in the academic department are staying with him. A loyal and devoted disciple of Dr. Riley's. "Well, you see it is this way, Dr. Riley." Believes that Tennyson should have said, not "In the spring, a young man's fancy, etc.," but rather "In the summer," when there's nothing else to do. Harry Bryan: "Going to town?"

name: Gillespie, Frank A.
motto: "His greatest ambition, we regret to state, Is simply this, to graduate."
description: The beginning of the end of a long line of Gillespies, for he leaves only Meek to hold up the family name at "Ole Miss." Merchant, Insurance Agent, Educator--what next? Fond of talking about a fair young Miss down home. His favorite occupation is hanging around the post-office waiting for that letter. Missed the picture show one night this year--it rained. "Boys, did my letter come?" "I'll bound you, she's a queen." "Tee-hee."
text:
"His greatest ambition, we regret to state, Is simply this, to graduate."
The beginning of the end of a long line of Gillespies, for he leaves only Meek to hold up the family name at "Ole Miss." Merchant, Insurance Agent, Educator--what next? Fond of talking about a fair young Miss down home. His favorite occupation is hanging around the post-office waiting for that letter. Missed the picture show one night this year--it rained. "Boys, did my letter come?" "I'll bound you, she's a queen." "Tee-hee."

name: Guy, T. Alcus
motto: "Litigious terms, fat contentions, and flowing fees."
description: Guy was here years ago. Tried his hand at teaching but the love for his Alma Mater drew him hither once more. As Manager of the Students' Co-Operative Boarding Club, he has proved his administrative ability. He and Dick have succeeded in materially reducing the board bill of their members--real constructive work. A frequent visitor to "the coop," he is a favorite among the ladies.
text:
"Litigious terms, fat contentions, and flowing fees."
Guy was here years ago. Tried his hand at teaching but the love for his Alma Mater drew him hither once more. As Manager of the Students' Co-Operative Boarding Club, he has proved his administrative ability. He and Dick have succeeded in materially reducing the board bill of their members--real constructive work. A frequent visitor to "the coop," he is a favorite among the ladies.

name: Ham, Mark
motto: "We have met the enemy and they are ours."
description: Look at his photo again. Now you wouldn't think he was an Indian fighter, would you? Well he deceives his looks for he once whipped a whole tribe. That's the reason those Wild West shows pass Oxford up. Now that he leaves us our poor little Freshmen will once more have an opportunity to see real Indians. He hails from the heart of the Delta and looks upon Freshmen just as all those Delta folks do on niggers. Mark is a good football player, a social light and his favorite studies are--wait, I will run ask him.
text:

Mark is a good football player, a social light and his favorite studies are--wait, I will run ask him.

name: Hardy, Eugene Jennings
motto: "I hate nobody; I am in charity with the world."
description: "Gene" never worries; his troubles are his follies. No man is more generally liked than he. There is no power to retard the progress of one who is so self-sacrificing and hesitates at nothing to do kindness to others.
text:
"I hate nobody; I am in charity with the world."
"Gene" never worries; his troubles are his follies. No man is more generally liked than he. There is no power to retard the progress of one who is so self-sacrificing and hesitates at nothing to do kindness to others.

name: Leland, B. Harris
motto: "Ambition has no rest."
description: Finished in three years; Another one of those prodigies. He is getting through in three years by hitting it hard and steady all the way along. He spends the summers in leading the singing at camp meetings. Those who have heard him sing—and who has not—can testify as to his ability along this line, for he possesses a tin horn tenor of the most winning tone. A good student, a good Baptist, a good Democrat—what better attributes than these could a young Southerner have to assure for him a brilliant future?
text:
"Ambition has no rest."

name: William, Lake Hays
motto: "He is complete in feature and in mind, With all good grace to grace a gentleman."
clubs: Freshman and Sophomore medals in Oratory
"Ole Miss" representative at the Crystal Springs Chautauqua Oratorical Contest 1914
"Ole Miss" representative at the State Oratorical Contest 1915
description: It is very singular that this man from Walthall should be voted the biggest politician in school, for everything that he has obtained in the University has been gained through the medium of honesty and straightforwardness. Then we must deduce that he goes into the realms of politics on account of the pure love for the game. That the students have faith in his ability to go into the battles of the world is attested by his being voted the most likely to succeed. He has a winning personality, a genial bearing, and a genuine friendliness that draws one to him, and we part, saying that his industry is equal to his geniality.
text:

name: James, W. Kirkwood
motto: "We thank the Gods our Rome has such a soldier."
description: Known among his friends as Lady-Killer. Has an inherited fondness for out-door sports. Will go miles to get to sit on the bank of a stream and try his luck at snagging the finny tribe. Never wearies of tramping the fields and hills behind his trusty dog in search of game. "Kirk" returns next year for his B. E., and every good wish of our class goes with him as he enters upon his life work.
text:
"We thank the Gods our Rome has such a soldier."
Known among his friends as Lady-Killer. Has an inherited fondness for out-door sports. Will go miles to get to sit on the bank of a stream and try his luck at snagging the finny tribe. Never wearies of tramping the fields and hills behind his trusty dog in search of game. "Kirk" returns next year for his B. E., and every good wish of our class goes with him as he enters upon his life work.

name: William, Robert Lott
motto: "This is the thing that I was born to do."
description: He came to Mississippi from Millsaps, and as usual with their representatives he has shown up well. The Meds speak of him as one of their best, and the student body as a whole has found him to be a quiet and genial kind of a fellow. We congratulate next year's medical class that they, too, will get the benefit of his good fellowship. Prof. Rogers: "It's a mighty big subject."
text:
"This is the thing that I was born to do."
He came to Mississippi from Millsaps, and as usual with their representatives he has shown up well. The Meds speak of him as one of their best, and the student body as a whole has found him to be a quiet and genial kind of a fellow. We congratulate next year's medical class that they, too, will get the benefit of his good fellowship. Prof. Rogers: "It's a mighty big subject."

name: McBryde, Annie
motto: "Knowledge and truth and virtue were her theme."
description: History, Math, Latin—what taste! Won a Taylor Medal in Math, and has been smiling ever since. Heard that a nobler Romaness had appeared and made a return trip to "Ole Miss" just to show her up. Believes that there is no place like Columbia, Tennessee, unless it be Goodman, Mississippi. Given to telling wondrous yarns of the piscatorial variety. And she's some golfer, too.
text:
"Knowledge and truth and virtue were her theme."
History, Math, Latin—what taste! Won a Taylor Medal in Math, and has been smiling ever since. Heard that a nobler Romaness had appeared and made a return trip to "Ole Miss" just to show her up. Believes that there is no place like Columbia, Tennessee, unless it be Goodman, Mississippi. Given to telling wondrous yarns of the piscatorial variety. And she's some golfer, too.

name: Owen, Stanford E.
motto: "Talks as familiarly of roaring lions As maids of thirteen do of puppy dogs."
description: Little "Bloody" was not satisfied with one degree from "Ole Miss," so he came back this year to get a Masters. Taking into consideration his earnest consistent work in the class-room and his untiring efforts on the Athletic field we unhesitatingly say that he has that grit and determination which go to make up success.
text:
"Talks as familiarly of roaring lions As maids of thirteen do of puppy dogs."
Little "Bloody" was not satisfied with one degree from "Ole Miss," so he came back this year to get a Masters. Taking into consideration his earnest consistent work in the class-room and his untiring efforts on the Athletic field we unhesitatingly say that he has that grit and determination which go to make up success.

name: Pegues, Juliet McKie
motto: "There is none like her, none."
description: Walks as fast as she talks. Now if you have heard her talk you can judge at about what rate she is going when she passes the monument ten minutes late for an eight o'clock class. Likes Freshmen and young profs especially. Favorite studies, Latin, German, and Zoology. Has the distinction of making above ninety under "Buck," and that, too, when the "Mortality List" was unusually high. "Nuf sed" as to her ability as a student. Has a pleasant word and a smile for everybody under all circumstances. Yes, even when all the boards in the class-room are covered with examination questions.
text:
"There is none like her, none."
Walks as fast as she talks. Now if you have heard her talk you can judge at about what rate she is going when she passes the monument ten minutes late for an eight o'clock class. Likes Freshmen and young profs especially. Favorite studies, Latin, German, and Zoology. Has the distinction of making above ninety under "Buck," and that, too, when the "Mortality List" was unusually high. "Nuf sed" as to her ability as a student. Has a pleasant word and a smile for everybody under all circumstances. Yes, even when all the boards in the class-room are covered with examination questions.

name: Potter, Chalmers
motto: "The rule of my life is to make business a pleasure, and pleasure my business."
description: Chalmers first considered Millsaps the logical school. But after spending one year there he saw that Ole Miss offered better opportunities for a man of his ability. He, too, was a member of that august body, the Court of St. James, and it has left its impress upon him. Now he has become a professor in Physics and is addressed as Dr. Potter by such as do not wish to take Physics Lab. two years. In his youth he was rather wild, even being on the Prom Committee, but now he has settled down and no one is more dignified than he.
text:

Chalmers first considered Millsaps the logical school. But after spending one year there he saw that Ole Miss offered better opportunities for a man of his ability. In his youth he was rather wild, even being on the Prom Committee, but now he has settled down and no one is more dignified than he.

name: Rechtin, W. Henry
motto: "Wise to resolve, and patient to perform."
description: Spends most of his time trying to concoct some chemical mixture more foul smelling than those of the immortal Rube. He and his accomplices furnish substantial argument in favor of moving the Chemistry Department clear off the campus. One of the Amis-Rechtin Mississippian Staff which made our paper one of the best in the South.
text:
"Wise to resolve, and patient to perform."

He and his accomplices furnish substantial argument in favor of moving the Chemistry Department clear off the campus. One of the Amis-Rechtin Mississippian Staff which made our paper one of the best in the South.

name: Rechtin, J. Theodore
motto: "Good humor only teaches charms to last, Still makes new conquests and maintains the past."
description: Has attempted not without some degree of success, to include every course in the University in his schedule. A very bent for heavy courses. "Big Rech" is sure to make his mark as a teacher of languages both Ancient and Modern, provided the "Braves" don't find themselves in need of a "port-sider." A whole-souled, good-natured chap who leaves many friends at University.
text:
"Good humor only teaches charms to last, Still makes new conquests and maintains the past."
Has attempted not without some degree of success, to include every course in the University in his schedule. A very bent for heavy courses. "Big Rech" is sure to make his mark as a teacher of languages both Ancient and Modern, provided the "Braves" don't find themselves in need of a "port-sider." A whole-souled, good-natured chap who leaves many friends at University.

name: Robinson, Walter W.
motto: "Nothing is impossible to industry."
description: Twice voted the best and hardest student, and that, too, all but unanimously. For at application and industry, Flip has no peer. We bar one, and he has the marks to show for his "boning" - and what is more, the knowledge. There is no part of the human anatomy - whether it be bone, muscle, or nerve - that he is not "savvy" about. Such natural ability and capacity for work is bound to place Flip among the "highbrows."
text:
"Nothing is impossible to industry."
Twice voted the best and hardest student, and that, too, all but unanimously. For at application and industry, Flip has no peer. We bar one, and he has the marks to show for his "boning" - and what is more, the knowledge. There is no part of the human anatomy - whether it be bone, muscle, or nerve - that he is not "savvy" about. Such natural ability and capacity for work is bound to place Flip among the "highbrows."

name: Schloss, Charles M.
motto: "Whence is thy learning? Hath thou toil Over pond'rous books, consume the midnight oil?"
description: Yes, he must burn the midnight oil for such recitations as he gives would be impossible otherwise. Studious, good-natured, artistic, "Schloss" is a favorite among both students and professors. Responsible for most of the drawings in this book, he has performed his task well in keeping with his customary thoroughness. But his student days have not all been smooth sailing, for too frequently as a freshman he was a defendant at the Court of St. James. "Freshman, root that dime out!" He goes from us to Boston Tech, where he is sure to be a leader in his life work, Electrical Engineering.
text:

He goes from us to Boston Tech, where he is sure to be a leader in his life work, Electrical Engineering.

name: Scott, Clyde Kell
motto: "Doubt truth to be a liar, But never doubt I love."
clubs: Chairman Social Committee Y. W. C. A. 1913-14
description: A living refutation of the maxim that environment determines character. Just to show her utter contempt for the sciences she has taken a B. A. contrary to what one might naturally have expected. Combines the qualities of a good student and a social favorite—a combination too rarely found. Fond of lecturing Freshman Co-Eds on "How to keep Zoological specimens away from the ants." Her favorite amusement is skating in front of the Library.
text:
"Doubt truth to be a liar, But never doubt I love."
A living refutation of the maxim that environment determines character. Just to show her utter contempt for the sciences she has taken a B. A. contrary to what one might naturally have expected. Combines the qualities of a good student and a social favorite—a combination too rarely found. Fond of lecturing Freshman Co-Eds on "How to keep Zoological specimens away from the ants." Her favorite amusement is skating in front of the Library.

name: Smythe, Frank W.
motto: "A faultless body and a blameless mind."
description: We would not cope with the athletic sheets in the praises of the prowess of this man, for it seems they have left nothing unsaid. He has baffled "Joe" as well as opposing ends, and has shown himself to be a student as well as an athlete. He has proved the saying that one will be repaid in terms of the effort put forth; and with two years more he will indeed be a doctor.
text:
"A faultless body and a blameless mind."
We would not cope with the athletic sheets in the praises of the prowess of this man, for it seems they have left nothing unsaid. He has baffled "Joe" as well as opposing ends, and has shown himself to be a student as well as an athlete. He has proved the saying that one will be repaid in terms of the effort put forth; and with two years more he will indeed be a doctor.

name: Solomon, David R.
motto: "Night after night, He sat and bleared his eyes with books."
description: One of the many Meridian boys who have made their mark at Ole Miss. He is a student in the true sense of the word—even Prof. Torrey's Freshman Math did not stop him. His flowing pen secured for him his first year a place on the Mississippian staff; in his Junior year he was Editor-in-Chief, and was instrumental in publishing one of the best college papers in the South. This year he decided that he was not suited for the Academic life—now he is wrestling with Blackstone.
text:
"Night after night, He sat and bleared his eyes with books."
One of the many Meridian boys who have made their mark at Ole Miss. He is a student in the true sense of the word—even Prof. Torrey's Freshman Math did not stop him. His flowing pen secured for him his first year a place on the Mississippian staff; in his Junior year he was Editor-in-Chief, and was instrumental in publishing one of the best college papers in the South. This year he decided that he was not suited for the Academic life—now he is wrestling with Blackstone.

name: Stanford, J. Ellis
motto: "Am I not a smoker and a brother?"
description: One who appreciates opportunities, and admires truth and honesty. Easy, placid and reserved are his traits. A pipe, a warm fire, plenty of work and he is content. Knows how to give and take a good joke.
text:
"Am I not a smoker and a brother?"
One who appreciates opportunities, and admires truth and honesty. Easy, placid and reserved are his traits. A pipe, a warm fire, plenty of work and he is content. Knows how to give and take a good joke.

name: Kincannon, Taylor
motto: "my girl is a rectangular parallelepiped."
text: "my girl is a rectangular parallelepiped."

name: Tarver, W. Victor
motto: "Smooth runs the water where the brook is deep."
description: Sorry this picture is not made in colors because it doesn't do "Bricks" justice like it is. And you should have seen those locks after April 1, during his Freshman year! He lacked the necessary quarter to have them trimmed up in style, so his room-mate says. A hater of the lighter pleasures until his Senior year, when he discovered that he had two feet, and ever since he has been worshipping at Terpsichore's shrine. Never misses a hop. Also he's some "diner out." Oratorically inclined, he is a great admirer of Dr. Milden, Demosthenes, and Wall Doxey. "Ich habe es gefunden."
text:
"Smooth runs the water where the brook is deep."
Sorry this picture is not made in colors because it doesn't do "Bricks" justice like it is. And you should have seen those locks after April 1, during his Freshman year! He lacked the necessary quarter to have them trimmed up in style, so his room-mate says. A hater of the lighter pleasures until his Senior year, when he discovered that he had two feet, and ever since he has been worshipping at Terpsichore's shrine. Never misses a hop. Also he's some "diner out." Oratorically inclined, he is a great admirer of Dr. Milden, Demosthenes, and Wall Doxey. "Ich habe es gefunden."

name: Unger, James Kelly
motto: "Rare on earth is such a constant prep found."
clubs: Elected to Y. M. C. A. Conference 1913 at Black Mountain, N. C.
description: The quotation is highly appropriate, for he is Prince of all Preps and can make more bum puns in a minute than any other man on the campus. As preacher, missionary, and Sunday School teacher, he goes about to the neighboring churches doing good. But catch him in his lighter moods, and a jollier and livelier fellow to spend an hour with cannot be found. He goes to Africa as a Missionary, so he says—but we think that his real intention in going is to learn more about negro life; and ere long the Orpheum billboards will be heralding the coming of "Kelly Unger," Celebrated Negro Comedian.
text:
"Rare on earth is such a constant prep found."
The quotation is highly appropriate, for he is Prince of all Preps and can make more bum puns in a minute than any other man on the campus. As preacher, missionary, and Sunday School teacher, he goes about to the neighboring churches doing good. But catch him in his lighter moods, and a jollier and livelier fellow to spend an hour with cannot be found. He goes to Africa as a Missionary, so he says—but we think that his real intention in going is to learn more about negro life; and ere long the Orpheum billboards will be heralding the coming of "Kelly Unger," Celebrated Negro Comedian.

name: Wilks, Zeno E.
motto: "Take-it-easy and Live-long are brothers."
description: Someone has said that genius is the capacity for hard work. Of course he meant when hard work is necessary. This being true, Zeno should undoubtedly be classed as a genius, for he is carrying thirty-two hours work in his Senior year. But even though Torrey's Math and Kennon's Physics are included in those thirty-two hours, he is a living example of one who takes things easy. A more genial, pleasant and friendly man we have never met.
text:
"Take-it-easy and Live-long are brothers."
Someone has said that genius is the capacity for hard work. Of course he meant when hard work is necessary. This being true, Zeno should undoubtedly be classed as a genius, for he is carrying thirty-two hours work in his Senior year. But even though Torrey's Math and Kennon's Physics are included in those thirty-two hours, he is a living example of one who takes things easy. A more genial, pleasant and friendly man we have never met.

name: Wilson, Abner Thomas
motto: "I have learned in whatsoever state I am, therewith to be content."
description: One of Oxford's sons of whom she has reason to be proud. Rides over to the campus every morning to show the Profs up; then rides back home in the afternoon to prepare for the next day's conquest. Has the qualities that make for the best and most useful in life—earnestness of purpose and a determination to succeed. A poet and short story writer of real worth; and the week seldom passes that the Mississippian does not carry a contribution fresh from his gifted pen. He is especially fond of the classics and philosophy, as his work in these departments clearly shows.
text:
"I have learned in whatsoever state I am, therewith to be content."
One of Oxford's sons of whom she has reason to be proud. Rides over to the campus every morning to show the Profs up; then rides back home in the afternoon to prepare for the next day's conquest. Has the qualities that make for the best and most useful in life—earnestness of purpose and a determination to succeed. A poet and short story writer of real worth; and the week seldom passes that the Mississippian does not carry a contribution fresh from his gifted pen. He is especially fond of the classics and philosophy, as his work in these departments clearly shows.

name: Boone, Everett Edward
motto: "Let us consider the reasons of the case, for nothing is law that is not reason."
description: Strange to say a good man like Boone never discovered that there is a good place like "Ole Miss" till this year, he having acquired his literary education at Mississippi College and L. S. U. A better student or clearer thinker is rarely ever found and we feel safe in saying that no "dip" this year will represent more consistent hard work than Boone's.
text:
"Let us consider the reasons of the case, for nothing is law that is not reason."
Strange to say a good man like Boone never discovered that there is a good place like "Ole Miss" till this year, he having acquired his literary education at Mississippi College and L. S. U. A better student or clearer thinker is rarely ever found and we feel safe in saying that no "dip" this year will represent more consistent hard work than Boone's.

name: Breland, J. J.
motto: "A man is the whole encyclopedia of facts."
description: "Si" is active in every phase of college life. On the football field when a gain is needed the ball goes to "Si." He is always on hand at meetings of Phi Sigma and the Blackstone Club, and, if he gets the floor the other members hear oratory such as has not been heard since the days of Joe Bailey. Lastly, "Si" is always there when it comes to putting up a recitation in the classroom.
text:
"A man is the whole encyclopedia of facts."
"Si" is active in every phase of college life. On the football field when a gain is needed the ball goes to "Si." He is always on hand at meetings of Phi Sigma and the Blackstone Club, and, if he gets the floor the other members hear oratory such as has not been heard since the days of Joe Bailey. Lastly, "Si" is always there when it comes to putting up a recitation in the classroom.

name: Case, Hal Franklin
motto: "The noblest Roman of them all."
description: Hal came to us from a sister school to enter our law department, and we are proud when we can enroll such men. He has the knack, the will, and the brains to work, which will make a winning combination out in the cold, hard world.
text:
"The noblest Roman of them all."
Hal came to us from a sister school to enter our law department, and we are proud when we can enroll such men. He has the knack, the will, and the brains to work, which will make a winning combination out in the cold, hard world.

name: Chandler, Lane Terrell
motto: "A friend may well be reckoned a masterpiece of nature."
description: Sober, wise and just, from this fellow we may expect much. As a pitcher for four years on the "Ole Miss" varsity and one year in the Southern and Virginia leagues, "Jay Bird" has won the admiration of all who have seen him in the box. He has also won the admiration of every student at the University yet he carries his accomplishments as modestly as a maid. A sound discretion and a strong intellect supported by an earnest and upright character, assures for him high attainment.
text:
"A friend may well be reckoned a masterpiece of nature."
Sober, wise and just, from this fellow we may expect much. As a pitcher for four years on the "Ole Miss" varsity and one year in the Southern and Virginia leagues, "Jay Bird" has won the admiration of all who have seen him in the box. He has also won the admiration of every student at the University yet he carries his accomplishments as modestly as a maid. A sound discretion and a strong intellect supported by an earnest and upright character, assures for him high attainment.

name: Holmes, D. W.
motto: "The height of that woman's ambition is to be Mrs. D. W. Holmes."
text: "The height of that woman's ambition is to be Mrs. D. W. Holmes."

name: Dinsmore, John R.
motto: "Life is not so short but there is always time for courtesy."
description: "Chonsey" is always on the jump and is the busiest man on the campus, a natural leader of men, a friend to all. If any one is in trouble or sorrow John is the first to sympathize and aid; he has a great many qualities that do not come out of books, not the least of which is good fellowship and he goes out from us a favorite with boys and girls alike.
text:
"Life is not so short but there is always time for courtesy."
"Chonsey" is always on the jump and is the busiest man on the campus, a natural leader of men, a friend to all. If any one is in trouble or sorrow John is the first to sympathize and aid; he has a great many qualities that do not come out of books, not the least of which is good fellowship and he goes out from us a favorite with boys and girls alike.

name: Ford, Paul E.
motto: "Knowledge is power."
description: Very modestly carries the honor of being as good as Judge Kimbrough's Equity-mill can produce. Has repeatedly represented us in tilts on the rostrum with our ancient rivals, the sisters of Ole Miss, A. & M., Millsaps and Clinton, and each time we have had reason to be proud of our choice. Has succeeded in sustaining the enviable record of his family name—Eb, Joe, Harry, Rossie, and Paul. We have known them all, but none has impressed us more favorably than this red-headed lawyer. Not related to Dr. Heddleston's car.
text:
"Knowledge is power."
Very modestly carries the honor of being as good as Judge Kimbrough's Equity-mill can produce. Has repeatedly represented us in tilts on the rostrum with our ancient rivals, the sisters of Ole Miss, A. & M., Millsaps and Clinton, and each time we have had reason to be proud of our choice. Has succeeded in sustaining the enviable record of his family name—Eb, Joe, Harry, Rossie, and Paul. We have known them all, but none has impressed us more favorably than this red-headed lawyer. Not related to Dr. Heddleston's car.

name: Gautier, H. W.
motto: "Long and tall, huge-boned, grim and gaunt."
description: This is a man who commands the admiration of all who know him. We admire him most for his natural ability to lead men. Add to his natural abilities two degrees and a keen insight into human nature, and "Wampus" is sure to be a winner. He stands six feet seven and is every inch a man.
text:
"Long and tall, huge-boned, grim and gaunt."
This is a man who commands the admiration of all who know him. We admire him most for his natural ability to lead men. Add to his natural abilities two degrees and a keen insight into human nature, and "Wampus" is sure to be a winner. He stands six feet seven and is every inch a man.

name: Gilley, Frank Dowd
high school: Aberdeen High, Castle Heights, Georgia Military Academy, Georgia Tech
motto: "I'm as sober as a judge."
description: "Gilly" wears the weight of learning with the pomp of a judge. It never can be said of him that he was "bethumped of words" and we are ardent in our belief that in him the state has a practical factor to be dealt with. Judge Farley: "It ain't worth a barbee."
text:
"I'm as sober as a judge."
"Gilly" wears the weight of learning with the pomp of a judge. It never can be said of him that he was "bethumped of words" and we are ardent in our belief that in him the state has a practical factor to be dealt with. Judge Farley: "It ain't worth a barbee."

name: Hall, Lee Davis
motto: "Bid me discourse, I will enchant thine ear, Or like a fairy, trip upon the green."
clubs: President Board of Control, Co-operative Boarding Club
description: Lee came to us from Mississippi College. At last he found Mississippi's seat of learning, and his coming added one more good man to the University's long list of honored sons. From his work in the law class and his work in the moot court there is no doubt but that he will soon stand first among the bar of which Mississippi is justly proud.
text:
"Bid me discourse, I will enchant thine ear, Or like a fairy, trip upon the green."
Lee came to us from Mississippi College. At last he found Mississippi's seat of learning, and his coming added one more good man to the University's long list of honored sons. From his work in the law class and his work in the moot court there is no doubt but that he will soon stand first among the bar of which Mississippi is justly proud.

name: Hays, W. L.
motto: "Born for success, he seemed With grace to win, with heart to hold, With shining gifts that took all eyes."
clubs: Freshman and Sophomore Medals in Oratory
"Ole Miss" representative in the State Oratorical Contest 1915
description: Deservedly voted by the majority of the student body the most likely to succeed. To the long list of honors given above we want to add by way of characterization that our Editor-in-chief is courteous, energetic, and loyal to his friends. No man has been in such close touch with every man on the campus. He knows everybody and with his cheerful disposition is quick in converting new acquaintances into lasting friends.
text:
"Born for success, he seemed With grace to win, with heart to hold, With shining gifts that took all eyes."
Deservedly voted by the majority of the student body the most likely to succeed. To the long list of honors given above we want to add by way of characterization that our Editor-in-chief is courteous, energetic, and loyal to his friends. No man has been in such close touch with every man on the campus. He knows everybody and with his cheerful disposition is quick in converting new acquaintances into lasting friends.

name: Hightower, George
motto: "The Common Law itself is nothing more than reason."
description: Has been here since the Paleolithic Age. For one year he deserted us for A. & M., but we flatter ourselves in believing that his sole reason for doing so was that it was Dad's school. Phlegmatic and deliberate, he has all the marks of a Southern gentleman; faultlessly neat in his personal appearance and a very Chesterfield in his manners. George is a good fellow and has the personal charm that distinguishes him as a man among men.
text:
"The Common Law itself is nothing more than reason."
Has been here since the Paleolithic Age. For one year he deserted us for A. & M., but we flatter ourselves in believing that his sole reason for doing so was that it was Dad's school. Phlegmatic and deliberate, he has all the marks of a Southern gentleman; faultlessly neat in his personal appearance and a very Chesterfield in his manners. George is a good fellow and has the personal charm that distinguishes him as a man among men.

name: Hudson, J. K.
motto: "Never idle a moment, but thrifty and thoughtful of others."
description: Jim Kyle has all those qualities which go to make up a finished, well-rounded man and lawyer. As a speaker in the Blackstone Club he has proved himself equal to any occasion, prepared or extemporaneous. As a member of the University Bar he can cite as many pointed authorities and put up as convincing argument as any other man in the class. As a student he has a record of which anyone should be envious.
text:
"Never idle a moment, but thrifty and thoughtful of others."
Jim Kyle has all those qualities which go to make up a finished, well-rounded man and lawyer. As a speaker in the Blackstone Club he has proved himself equal to any occasion, prepared or extemporaneous. As a member of the University Bar he can cite as many pointed authorities and put up as convincing argument as any other man in the class. As a student he has a record of which anyone should be envious.

name: Kincannon, Jim Clayte
motto: "Lest men suspect your tale untrue, Keep probability in view."
description: When it is a question of application or a reward or a permit, "chance" is the only original. He is the kind that gets there even though he has to dig for it. It will only be a question of time until our state will have a lawyer worth while, in the person of Jim Clayte.
text:
"Lest men suspect your tale untrue, Keep probability in view."
When it is a question of application or a reward or a permit, "chance" is the only original. He is the kind that gets there even though he has to dig for it. It will only be a question of time until our state will have a lawyer worth while, in the person of Jim Clayte.

name: Leftwich, George J., Jr.
motto: "A soul of power, a well of lofty thought, with tongue of silver and frame of iron."
description: Here is a man rich in experience, fresh from the fields of Aberdeen. "Jay Bez" has the straightforward directness and simple honesty, rare today, that men seize upon when offered. His work in the classroom is but a forerunner of the fact that George will, within a few years, stand high in the Mississippi bar.
text:
"A soul of power, a well of lofty thought, with tongue of silver and frame of iron."
Here is a man rich in experience, fresh from the fields of Aberdeen. "Jay Bez" has the straightforward directness and simple honesty, rare today, that men seize upon when offered. His work in the classroom is but a forerunner of the fact that George will, within a few years, stand high in the Mississippi bar.

name: Lewis, Charles Dana
motto: "In alle the ordres foure is non thut kan So muche of dailaunce and faire language."
major: LL. B., B. A. Mississippi College
description: Behold the famous pinch hitter! Made his reputation at Ole Miss as a heavy hitter on the 1914 Varsity. His lasting reputation, however, was made at Mississippi College as Orator, Athlete and Class President. Has also been a teacher in the Philippines and is such an extensive traveler that he is undecided as to whether to practice his chosen profession in Alaska or Argentina. A ladies' man of no mean ability he spends six evenings a week at Rick's Hall.
text:
"In alle the ordres foure is non thut kan So muche of dailaunce and faire language."
Behold the famous pinch hitter! Made his reputation at Ole Miss as a heavy hitter on the 1914 Varsity. His lasting reputation, however, was made at Mississippi College as Orator, Athlete and Class President. Has also been a teacher in the Philippines and is such an extensive traveler that he is undecided as to whether to practice his chosen profession in Alaska or Argentina. A ladies' man of no mean ability he spends six evenings a week at Rick's Hall.

name: McCraw, Ambrose Benjamin
motto: "There is likewise a reward for faithful silence."
major: LL. B., B. S. Degree from Central Normal College, Indiana
description: "Mac" is one of those fellows who says but little, though when he speaks, something has been said. He possesses a grim determination, and this, coupled with his other many attributes, predicts for him a place high in his profession.
text:
"There is likewise a reward for faithful silence."
"Mac" is one of those fellows who says but little, though when he speaks, something has been said. He possesses a grim determination, and this, coupled with his other many attributes, predicts for him a place high in his profession.

name: Morse, W. E.
motto: "Whose body lodged a mighty mind."
clubs: Chairman of Y. M. C. A. Reading Room
A. & M. College Debater at Columbus
Board of Control of the University Co-operative Boarding Club
Board of Control of the Athletic Association
Board of Control of the Mississippian
President of the Hermaean Literary Society, First Term
description: Millsaps never sent us a better man. As librarian, litterateur, and leader in all student activities, "Gene" has impressed us all with his extraordinary natural gifts and sound scholarship. Jumped into the limelight down home as bailiff in the Bilbo trial. Watch our prediction—he will find his deserved place at the very top, and that too, quite soon.
text:
"Whose body lodged a mighty mind."
Millsaps never sent us a better man. As librarian, litterateur, and leader in all student activities, "Gene" has impressed us all with his extraordinary natural gifts and sound scholarship. Jumped into the limelight down home as bailiff in the Bilbo trial. Watch our prediction—he will find his deserved place at the very top, and that too, quite soon.

name: Puryear, Leon P.
motto: "Fear not my lord, I will play the orator, As if the golden fee for which I plead were for myself."
description: Keep your eyes open, Demosthenes, Puryear will get your reputation if you don't watch out. When it comes to "orating" we give it to him. He can think of more flowery language, and say it with more convincing earnestness than Patrick Henry ever dared—we have reference to speaking before audiences of more than one, but this characteristic trait is undoubtedly still there when the audience is only one, the light burning low, and the hour late.
text:
"Fear not my lord, I will play the orator, As if the golden fee for which I plead were for myself."
Keep your eyes open, Demosthenes, Puryear will get your reputation if you don't watch out. When it comes to "orating" we give it to him. He can think of more flowery language, and say it with more convincing earnestness than Patrick Henry ever dared—we have reference to speaking before audiences of more than one, but this characteristic trait is undoubtedly still there when the audience is only one, the light burning low, and the hour late.

name: White, M. E.
motto: "Work first, and then rest."
clubs: Business Manager U. of M. Magazine, '10-'11
description: "Silver City" certainly has those qualities from which success naturally flows. He is a solid man physically as is evidenced by his consistent bucking the line on the Scrub football team. Nor does he fall short mentally, for in the classroom he stands always well in the lead.
text:
"Work first, and then rest."
"Silver City" certainly has those qualities from which success naturally flows. He is a solid man physically as is evidenced by his consistent bucking the line on the Scrub football team. Nor does he fall short mentally, for in the classroom he stands always well in the lead.

name: Young, Bessie
motto: "The countenance is the portrait of the soul, and the eyes mark its intentions."
description: Proud? Why certainly the Senior law class is proud of its only Co-ed. The only thing we're sorry of is that she didn't find out what a good place this is until this year. Indiana's loss is truly our gain. Her cheerful smile and kindly spirit, like oases in the desert of Pomeroy, and her thorough preparedness for recitations will always be an inspiration to, and command the respect of, the entire class.
text:
"The countenance is the portrait of the soul, and the eyes mark its intentions."
Proud? Why certainly the Senior law class is proud of its only Co-ed. The only thing we're sorry of is that she didn't find out what a good place this is until this year. Indiana's loss is truly our gain. Her cheerful smile and kindly spirit, like oases in the desert of Pomeroy, and her thorough preparedness for recitations will always be an inspiration to, and command the respect of, the entire class.

name: Bender, Chas. A.
motto: "The finest fruit earth holds up to its Maker is a finished man."
description: "Chief" is one of those royal good fellows who is always willing to play well a man's part. To name his most admirable trait would be difficult for they are too numerous. On the football field he was one of the first to make the gains, and likewise on the baseball diamond and the basketball court. In the class room he was first to answer. In society's game he always drew a winning hand. He is truly an all-around college man.
text:
"The finest fruit earth holds up to its Maker is a finished man."
"Chief" is one of those royal good fellows who is always willing to play well a man's part. To name his most admirable trait would be difficult for they are too numerous. On the football field he was one of the first to make the gains, and likewise on the baseball diamond and the basketball court. In the class room he was first to answer. In society's game he always drew a winning hand. He is truly an all-around college man.

name: Brunson, C. W.
motto: "When I was sick you gave me bitter pills.", "Laugh at your friends and if your friends are sore so much the better, you may laugh the more."
description: Mr. "Bones" is steady and dependable whether he be in Dr. Crider's examination room, on the football field, or wearing a claw hammer at a social function. Boisterous and affable and capable. Junior member of the firm of Bramlette and Brunson.
text:
"When I was sick you gave me bitter pills.", "Laugh at your friends and if your friends are sore so much the better, you may laugh the more."
Mr. "Bones" is steady and dependable whether he be in Dr. Crider's examination room, on the football field, or wearing a claw hammer at a social function. Boisterous and affable and capable. Junior member of the firm of Bramlette and Brunson.

name: Dean, S. R.
motto: "And what is thy history? A blank, my lord."
description: In his peculiar steady way he has ground out enough credits from both the science and medical departments to entitle him to two degrees. He has music in his soul and the advocates of the light fantastic will surely miss his wonderful impersonations of the great Paderewski. We know that any man with as much unselfishness as Simon will never fail in his chosen path of life.
text:
"And what is thy history? A blank, my lord."
In his peculiar steady way he has ground out enough credits from both the science and medical departments to entitle him to two degrees. He has music in his soul and the advocates of the light fantastic will surely miss his wonderful impersonations of the great Paderewski. We know that any man with as much unselfishness as Simon will never fail in his chosen path of life.

name: Dicks, John Barber
motto: "Thinking that nothing was done if anything remained to do."
description: Dicks came to us this year from Tulane, and since he has been with us he has truly made an enviable record in the class room. He is a man who attends strictly to his own business, and he has but little time for any proposition unless it is a business one.
text:
"Thinking that nothing was done if anything remained to do."
Dicks came to us this year from Tulane, and since he has been with us he has truly made an enviable record in the class room. He is a man who attends strictly to his own business, and he has but little time for any proposition unless it is a business one.

name: Hamm, Jeff
motto: "I'm telling you what's a fact."
text: "I'm telling you what's a fact."

name: Greene, Hosie C.
motto: "None knew thee but to love thee, None named thee but to praise."
description: Hosie is a man of reason. He deliberates at length sometimes, but when his mind has once been made up you may be sure he is on the right side. He has two more years in school and even though he is a married man, we predict that he will come out with flying colours and will some day rightfully command a high rank among the physicians of Mississippi.
text:
"None knew thee but to love thee, None named thee but to praise."
Hosie is a man of reason. He deliberates at length sometimes, but when his mind has once been made up you may be sure he is on the right side. He has two more years in school and even though he is a married man, we predict that he will come out with flying colours and will some day rightfully command a high rank among the physicians of Mississippi.

name: Harris, J. Harley
motto: "Though I am splenitive and rash, yet I have something in me dangerous."
description: "Pop came from a wild section of the country, and he has lived up to the worst stories we have ever heard from his neck of the woods. We were praying that this big chief of the Ku Klux would relieve us of his presence after this year, but he is coming back. I am glad I won't be a freshman next year."—By a Freshman. He is earnest, straightforward, and capable and has a bright future ahead of him.
text:
"Though I am splenitive and rash, yet I have something in me dangerous."
"Pop came from a wild section of the country, and he has lived up to the worst stories we have ever heard from his neck of the woods. We were praying that this big chief of the Ku Klux would relieve us of his presence after this year, but he is coming back. I am glad I won't be a freshman next year."—By a Freshman. He is earnest, straightforward, and capable and has a bright future ahead of him.

name: Jones, Quitman Allen
motto: "A quiet, thoughtful, good, sincere lad."
description: "Ooley" hails from McTyeire where he was even more illustrious in the athletic world than in the University—which is saying a lot. He made his M in baseball and basketball his first year at Ole Miss and if the elusive functions of the human anatomy had not required a great part of his time at the Science Hall he would have made somebody hump for quarter on the eleven. His only weakness is his friendliness and inability to do more for his friends. Student: "Shoot the skids!"
text:
"A quiet, thoughtful, good, sincere lad."
"Ooley" hails from McTyeire where he was even more illustrious in the athletic world than in the University—which is saying a lot. He made his M in baseball and basketball his first year at Ole Miss and if the elusive functions of the human anatomy had not required a great part of his time at the Science Hall he would have made somebody hump for quarter on the eleven. His only weakness is his friendliness and inability to do more for his friends. Student: "Shoot the skids!"

name: Roberts, A. L.
motto: "Ah! Joy is me, for into my luckless life a woman has come."
description: Roberts is a regular burner of the midnight oil, a persistent, thorough student. He is always in a hurry to perform the day's work allotted to him and with his untiring industry he will certainly attain success in his chosen profession.
text:
"Ah! Joy is me, for into my luckless life a woman has come."
Roberts is a regular burner of the midnight oil, a persistent, thorough student. He is always in a hurry to perform the day's work allotted to him and with his untiring industry he will certainly attain success in his chosen profession.

name: Rucker, R. B.
motto: "The hand that follows intellect can achieve."
description: "Bob" was forced to leave us in days gone by on account of his health, but coming back he has made good in his work and with his fellow students. A modest, simple friendliness that overflows to everybody and everything seems to be his distinguishing quality.
text:
"The hand that follows intellect can achieve."
"Bob" was forced to leave us in days gone by on account of his health, but coming back he has made good in his work and with his fellow students. A modest, simple friendliness that overflows to everybody and everything seems to be his distinguishing quality.

name: Speed, B. W.
motto: "His heart as far from fraud as Heaven from Earth."
description: He first went to Memphis to pursue his Medical course, but later came to us for the finishing touches. "Speedy" added one more honorable man to the student body and we gladly welcomed him. He leaves behind him a record that he will always be proud of.
text:
"His heart as far from fraud as Heaven from Earth."
He first went to Memphis to pursue his Medical course, but later came to us for the finishing touches. "Speedy" added one more honorable man to the student body and we gladly welcomed him. He leaves behind him a record that he will always be proud of.

name: Mulloy, Robert L.
description: His younger days or nights rather were spent roaming around the campus as a K. K. K., a terror to Freshmen. But during his Senior year the Faculty claimed him, and now his conduct is in every respect in keeping with the title of "Doctor." No longer does he break the stillness of night with his asinine calls, no longer is he a member of the famous Dabbs Quintette, no longer does he make those frequent trips "down the road," for feeling the dignity of his position, he must needs set a good example for his Freshmen.
text: His younger days or nights rather were spent roaming around the campus as a K. K. K., a terror to Freshmen. But during his Senior year the Faculty claimed him, and now his conduct is in every respect in keeping with the title of "Doctor." No longer does he break the stillness of night with his asinine calls, no longer is he a member of the famous Dabbs Quintette, no longer does he make those frequent trips "down the road," for feeling the dignity of his position, he must needs set a good example for his Freshmen.

name: Scott, O. A.
motto: "Who does his best his circumstance allows, Does well, acts nobly; angels could do no more."
description: Any man that can wrestle sufficient units from "Little Allie" to warrant a B. E. has certainly the grit and determination that counts. A network of railroads, canals and highways will doubtless be the footprints he will leave on the sands of time.
text:
"Who does his best his circumstance allows, Does well, acts nobly; angels could do no more."
Any man that can wrestle sufficient units from "Little Allie" to warrant a B. E. has certainly the grit and determination that counts. A network of railroads, canals and highways will doubtless be the footprints he will leave on the sands of time.

name: Simmons, Clarence
motto: "Oh, Why should life all labor be?"
description: "Little old man Simmons" is always determined in everything he enters,—it matters not whether 'tis as a judge in Freshman Court or out-figuring "Little Fes." He has but one desire and that is to do the thing well. A man of his determination cannot fail.
text:
"Oh, Why should life all labor be?"
"Little old man Simmons" is always determined in everything he enters,—it matters not whether 'tis as a judge in Freshman Court or out-figuring "Little Fes." He has but one desire and that is to do the thing well. A man of his determination cannot fail.

name: Word, Earl Roby
motto: "If you want to phone to Heaven, Or sink a shaft to hell, Just leave it to the engineer And he will do it well."
description: His physical make-up as portrayed above is a very prominent factor in the life of a successful engineer. He has the build, the desire, and the determination which is easily demonstrated by the way he has fought for his math. credits.
text:
"If you want to phone to Heaven, Or sink a shaft to hell, Just leave it to the engineer And he will do it well."
His physical make-up as portrayed above is a very prominent factor in the life of a successful engineer. He has the build, the desire, and the determination which is easily demonstrated by the way he has fought for his math. credits.

name: Kincannon, Katharine
motto: "My brother said so."
text: "My brother said so."

name: Burnett, D. T.
motto: "The heroes are not all six feet tall. Large souls may dwell in bodies small."
description: "Runt" is ever on the job and is the first out to defend the "Moosers" on the athletic field. He has manifested those unmistakable signs of a practical dispenser and we expect to see him prosper in his profession.
text:
"The heroes are not all six feet tall. Large souls may dwell in bodies small."
"Runt" is ever on the job and is the first out to defend the "Moosers" on the athletic field. He has manifested those unmistakable signs of a practical dispenser and we expect to see him prosper in his profession.

name: Caver, L. E.
motto: "Perseverance conquers all things."
description: He is another one of those men who goes into his work with but one aim: to do the best he can. Being one of the best students in the class, we are assured of his success.
text:
"Perseverance conquers all things."
He is another one of those men who goes into his work with but one aim: to do the best he can. Being one of the best students in the class, we are assured of his success.

name: Clark, T. E.
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."
description: Here we have a chemical phenom—one that can create the most horrible smells known to science. We will miss his experiments when we are on the second floor of the Lyceum after he is gone. Some day he will startle the world with one of his compounds.
text:
"None but himself can be his parallel."
Here we have a chemical phenom—one that can create the most horrible smells known to science. We will miss his experiments when we are on the second floor of the Lyceum after he is gone. Some day he will startle the world with one of his compounds.

name: Gore, W. M.
description: "Pete" went to Tulane for his first year's work, but discovered later that we had a better course here. Fortunately he has remarkable persuasive ability for he had the misfortune just after the snow fight to fall into the hands of a bunch of revengeful Sophs, who argued that every first-year man must be the victim of their scissors. But he won the argument, and still wears his hair a la LaFollette.
text: "Pete" went to Tulane for his first year's work, but discovered later that we had a better course here. Fortunately he has remarkable persuasive ability for he had the misfortune just after the snow fight to fall into the hands of a bunch of revengeful Sophs, who argued that every first-year man must be the victim of their scissors. But he won the argument, and still wears his hair a la LaFollette.

name: Henshaw, W. C.
motto: "The mildest manners, the gentlest heart."
description: "Renny" is one of those fellows that just gets next to you, congenial, true, merry. In the searching light of two years' association, as clean, as strong, and withal, as warm-hearted and jolly a man as we have met.
text:
"The mildest manners, the gentlest heart."
"Renny" is one of those fellows that just gets next to you, congenial, true, merry. In the searching light of two years' association, as clean, as strong, and withal, as warm-hearted and jolly a man as we have met.

name: Mcearley, C. R.
motto: "A quiet, thoughtful, good, sincere lad."
description: "Mc" is simply one of those quiet, studious fellows who attends to his own business. One would think that he should be a confirmed member of the bachelor's club, but you never can tell—he might be fooling you about how he stands with the ladies. In the laboratory he is noted for his wit and jolly good nature, and in the class room,—well, he gets the grades.
text:
"A quiet, thoughtful, good, sincere lad."
"Mc" is simply one of those quiet, studious fellows who attends to his own business. One would think that he should be a confirmed member of the bachelor's club, but you never can tell—he might be fooling you about how he stands with the ladies. In the laboratory he is noted for his wit and jolly good nature, and in the class room,—well, he gets the grades.

name: Rawls, Inman C.
motto: "Ambition is no cure for love."
description: Rawls was famous when he came here, for he hails from the town of Bilbo. He is a pleasant congenial fellow, but like many other Pharmacheutes he's hopelessly in love. When that letter doesn't come at the appointed time you can tell the change in him without his telling you the trouble. He's a quiet, industrious student and we predict a sure success for him in his profession.
text:
"Ambition is no cure for love."
Rawls was famous when he came here, for he hails from the town of Bilbo. He is a pleasant congenial fellow, but like many other Pharmacheutes he's hopelessly in love. When that letter doesn't come at the appointed time you can tell the change in him without his telling you the trouble. He's a quiet, industrious student and we predict a sure success for him in his profession.

name: Waggoner, D. T.
motto: "He is firm in his convictions."
description: Although this man has been with us but one year, he has established himself among us as being true to his beliefs. He walks as though he has some important place in view to reach and has but a few minutes to make the trip. He seldom speaks before he is spoken to, but when asked a question his answers show reason.
text:
"He is firm in his convictions."
Although this man has been with us but one year, he has established himself among us as being true to his beliefs. He walks as though he has some important place in view to reach and has but a few minutes to make the trip. He seldom speaks before he is spoken to, but when asked a question his answers show reason.

name: Watson, Virgil
description: A big, husky fellow who has made a success in athletics. He feels that he must keep in training by rolling pills the rest of his life. A good fellow with a heart in proportion to his size; he is one of the favorites on the trips—a leader in the fun-making.
text: A big, husky fellow who has made a success in athletics. He feels that he must keep in training by rolling pills the rest of his life. A good fellow with a heart in proportion to his size; he is one of the favorites on the trips—a leader in the fun-making.

name: Welch, W. D.
motto: "Steady and industrious."
description: This man is proud of his achievements and much reason has he to be. There are large possibilities marked by his sober thoughtful demeanor. Welch is a studious, industrious fellow and we feel safe in predicting a success for him in the practice of his chosen profession.
text:
"Steady and industrious."
This man is proud of his achievements and much reason has he to be. There are large possibilities marked by his sober thoughtful demeanor. Welch is a studious, industrious fellow and we feel safe in predicting a success for him in the practice of his chosen profession.

name: Wiygul, Frank M.
motto: "Pursuit of knowledge under difficulties."
description: "Wiggle" decided to become a "Bull Mooser" after his Freshman days, and he has been wrestling with the Pharmacopoeia and Dr. Faser ever since. Industrious, quiet, and reserved, Frank is sure to make good as the accomplice of the doctor and the undertaker. Methinks I see him now in the effort to make those ungodly pills look normal, or in trying to make one of those unmixable emulsions mix right: "Why didn't Dr. Quack prescribe this in capsule form?" Prof. Erwin: "Oh, er, I see."
text:
"Pursuit of knowledge under difficulties."
"Wiggle" decided to become a "Bull Mooser" after his Freshman days, and he has been wrestling with the Pharmacopoeia and Dr. Faser ever since. Industrious, quiet, and reserved, Frank is sure to make good as the accomplice of the doctor and the undertaker. Prof. Erwin: "Oh, er, I see."
